# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

tmp_file="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
processed_file="/tmp/myapp-$$.processed"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmp_file" "$processed_file"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

cat > "$tmp_file" <<'EOF'
alpha
beta
gamma
delta
EOF

echo "Using temp file: $tmp_file"

while IFS= read -r line; do
  printf '%s\n' "${line^^}"
done < "$tmp_file" > "$processed_file"

echo "Reusing temp file path: $tmp_file"

if [[ -f "$tmp_file" ]]; then
  line_count=$(wc -l < "$tmp_file")
  echo "Temp file line count: $line_count"
fi

echo "Processed results:"
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo "$line"
done < "$processed_file"

echo "Final pass reading original temp file again:"
sed 's/^/RAW: /' "$tmp_file"
